key_requirements: init_change currently always routes to sdd_workflow_restructure_input after creation
Phase skip target is post_clarifications_created — must be set directly in STATE.yaml
No new StatePhase variants needed; skip is handled inline in init_change, not a new state
Understand current phase enum and transition rules before modifying init_change routing
Restructure-input phase (phase 2) is what gets skipped for structured issues
restructure_service.rs and group_service.rs are the service files that may need to handle pre-populated groups
Output schema: groups array with id, issues, requirements, questions
init_change must replicate group creation logic that restructure_input normally does
Pre-clarifications phase (phase 3) is skipped for structured issues
Pre-clarifications output format: pre_clarifications.md with status: answered
init_change must write pre_clarifications.md files with status: answered for each group
groups_progress.pre_clarifications must be populated for all groups
Phase 7 (reference_context) is absorbed — structured issues include reference context inline
init_change must write reference_context.md artifacts and advance groups_progress.reference_context
Artifact must include written_by: artifact_cli marker for verification to pass
spec_plan entries must have main_spec_ref with subfolder path (min 4 components)
Phase skip target — init_change sets STATE.yaml phase to post_clarifications_created
Post-clarifications output format needed for any pre-population
groups_progress.post_clarifications may need to be populated
score issues enrich command is a new subcommand on the existing issues CLI
Existing IssueBackend trait and issue file format (YAML frontmatter + markdown body)
Issue file body contains markdown sections — parser reads these sections
Pattern: existing issues CLI subcommands (list, show, sync, create, update, close, find)
